statement: Semantic pipeline execution should start as a single Temporal workflow that fans out activities over evidence ids, but must support partitioning (continue-as-new or child workflows per chunk) if windows/pipelines grow enough to pressure workflow history limits.
rationale: A single workflow is simpler and easier to operate for bounded windows. Temporal guidance indicates child workflows are primarily useful to partition event history and avoid size limits; the engine should only adopt that complexity when window/pipeline sizes demand it.
keyAssumptions: Some pilot windows or future collections may exceed the comfortable history size for a single workflow.
We can chunk evidence ids deterministically without harming scientific comparability.
validationCriteria: Pipeline workflow can complete for typical windows without child workflows.
If evidence count exceeds a threshold, the workflow can safely continue-as-new or spawn chunk workflows without losing idempotency guarantees.
critiquePoints: Adds workflow-complexity pressure early if over-engineered; may be premature for pilot.
questions: What is the evidence-count threshold where history pressure becomes real for our workload?
